prompts: Using watercolors to introduce the Doppler effect can vividly illustrate the concept of frequency changes due to wave motion. When an object emitting waves moves, the frequency of the waves changes, leading to varying colors in a visual representation. If the object approaches, the wave frequencies compact, showing shorter wavelengths as blue shifts. Conversely, if the object recedes, the frequencies elongate, depicting longer wavelengths as red shifts. This colorful demonstration effectively highlights how motion influences wave behavior in the Doppler effect, engaging learners through visually dynamic and vibrant imagery.
